rulings.Case Title: DANIEL MURPHY et al VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, AUGUST 21, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT THORPE INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: Hearing required. Thorpe has shifted the burden of proof. Fossen's Declaration establishes that Thorpe was present at the Morro Bay plant. Murphy's deposition establishes that he was at the Morro Bay plant any where from 2 to 5 or more times and, on some of those occasions, worked with insulation. What is not clear is how plaintiff connects Murphy to Thorpe's insulation at the Morro Bay site and how plaintiff demonstrates that the Thorpe insulation contained asbestos. If not hearsay, Berg's testimony is admissible. Fults testimony has not been provided to the Court. Why is it that the deposition testimony of Berg and Fults was not disclosed by June 15, 2007? =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: KIMBERLY LEWIS VS. BETHEL AME CHURCH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Kimberly Lewis' Order To Show Cause Re Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, AUGUST 21, 2007, LINE 16. PLAINTIFF KIMBERLY LEWIS' ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER: THE RELIEF REQUESTED IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF CAN PROVE SHE RESIDED AT THE SUBJECT PROPERTY, IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFF IS ABLE TO PROVE THAT SHE WAS A TENANT BASED ON THE REPRESENTATIONS OF HER MOTHER, THAT ONLY THE MOTHER WAS THE TENANT. AS A CONSEQUENCE, SHE DID NOT HAVE A LANDLORD-TENANT RELATIONSHIP WITH THE OWNER OF THE PROPERTY. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JULIO MESTRE VS. NATHANIEL P FORD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, AUGUST 21, 2007, LINE 17. PLAINTIFF JULIO MESTRE'S Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Judgment: The motion to vacate the judgment is denied. The Court did review the record and exercised its independent judgment in concluding that the Director's decision to accept, in part, and to reject, in part, the arbitrator's decision was supported by the record and the terms of the MOU. Both paragraphs 315 (time limits for Step 4) and 316 (Director's decision) use the word "shall". The MOU does not differentiate between procedural and substantive rights when granting the Director the "discretion in accepting, modifying or rejecting" the arbitrator's decision after a Step 4 hearing. That being so, the record supports the Director's decision to terminate Mr. Mestre. =(302/PJM/PB)
